name: Underworld Goddess of the Closed World
name_jp: <ruby lang="jp"><rb>閉ザサレシ世界ノ冥神</rb><rp> (</rp><rt>サロス＝エレス・クルヌギアス</rt><rp>)</rp></ruby>
desc:
4+ Effect Monsters
You can also use 1 monster your opponent controls as material to Link Summon this card. If this card is Link Summoned: You can negate the effects of all face-up monsters your opponent currently controls. This Link Summoned card is unaffected by your opponent's activated effects, unless they target this card. Once per turn, when your opponent activates a card or effect that Special Summons a monster(s) from the GY (Quick Effect): You can negate the activation.
mnst_eff:
4+ Effect Monsters
You can also use 1 monster your opponent controls as material to Link Summon this card. If this card is Link Summoned: You can negate the effects of all face-up monsters your opponent currently controls. This Link Summoned card is unaffected by your opponent's activated effects, unless they target this card. Once per turn, when your opponent activates a card or effect that Special Summons a monster(s) from the GY (Quick Effect): You can negate the activation.
eff:
4+ Effect Monsters
You can also use 1 monster your opponent controls as material to Link Summon this card. If this card is Link Summoned: You can negate the effects of all face-up monsters your opponent currently controls. This Link Summoned card is unaffected by your opponent's activated effects, unless they target this card. Once per turn, when your opponent activates a card or effect that Special Summons a monster(s) from the GY (Quick Effect): You can negate the activation.

action: Uses cards on the opponent's field as Link Materials
mst: Negates the effects of your opponent's Effect Monsters
Unaffected by the opponent's Monster Cards
Unaffected by the opponent's Spell Cards
Unaffected by the opponent's Trap Cards
Negates the activation of your opponent's Effect Monster's effect
Negates the activation of your opponent's Spell Cards
Negates the activation of your opponent's Trap Cards
summoning: Requires Effect Monsters as Link Materials
